Module 2: The Business
Launch Formula, Part 3
!
Executive Summary
!
Business Launch Formula Example for My Initial Business
Initially I began teaching people about the stock market (without any
credentials) and started a newsletter that went out to 19 people. That 19
person list eventually grew into 80,000 people - how does this happen?
You focus on getting people to opt in - this should be the number one
metric you operate by. All along you must continue to test and tweak the
process to increase your opt in conversation rate.
The initial list was made up of people who were interested in investing.
Around the time it reach 1,600 people, a mentor offered a suggestion to
partner together and create a product for traders.
So we created a launch list for this by offering content on trading to the
investor newsletter (although didn’t call it that at the time). That first launch
ended up doing $1,650.
We continued doing launches after this and got better each time. We did
around 2-3 launches per year. Later on I discovered a key group on my list
and called them the “middle ground” - people that traded more often than
investors but less than the traders.
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 2 --

Created a launch for this from the “investor” and “trader” list to a new
launch list. This launch did $106,000, which was the first one over 6-
figures. We had very limited costs for this launch, and had JV’s.
All it took was responding the market demand…
Next, I came up with an idea to perform webinars - I realized people just
wanted to learn directly. Each list got fed into the webinar list and they were
sold to from there.The webinars were doing around $30,000.
From this webinar I created a Home Study Course - this again drove
everyone into this new course. Each time it was simply a matter of
responding to what the market wanted.
If you do this, it will lead to creating a portfolio of products and building a
relationship with your list. And as you know, this is what it’s all about in the
end.
!
Key Takeaway
Your focus should be on getting people to opt in
Listen closely to your list and find out exactly what it is they want
Create a portfolio of products and a successful business by building a
relationship with your list
!
